sourceRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
sourceRefs.title: 2020 Cell Research PRDM9/DSB fate PDF

paperRefs.title: Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and the fate of double-stranded breaks

claims.text: Newly generated H3K4me3 peaks in leptotene and mid-zygotene spermatocytes are largely PRDM9-associated and preferentially overlap DSB hotspots rather than promoter-like common H3K4me3 peaks.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 3, Results: Identification of PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3; Fig. 1 and Supplementary Figs. S2-S3
locatorRefs.summary: The paper reports high overlap between DSB hotspots and newly generated H3K4me3, PRDM9-binding overlap, and motif enrichment.

risks.summary: The first-pass artifact records published overlap and enrichment claims but does not independently rerun peak thresholds or motif statistics.
Some DSB and PRDM9 binding evidence depends on public datasets integrated by the authors.

claims.text: The majority of newly generated H3K4me3 signals are PRDM9-dependent; loss of Prdm9 removes these marks, whereas Spo11 or Dmc1 loss does not similarly abolish them.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 3, Results: direct test of Prdm9-/-, Spo11-/-, and Dmc1-/- spermatogenic cells
locatorRefs.summary: Prdm9 loss redirects DSB hotspots toward common H3K4me3 regions, while new H3K4me3 profiles remain similar in Spo11 and Dmc1 mutants.

risks.summary: The writer-dependence claim is stronger than downstream DSB fate causality; genotype effects may include broader meiotic changes.

claims.text: PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 is temporally dynamic: it begins in leptotene, peaks around mid-zygotene, then declines through pachytene, leading the authors to infer that many DSBs may form around mid-zygotene.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 3-4, Results: Dynamics and regulation of PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 signals; Fig. 2
locatorRefs.summary: Stage-resolved counts show H3K4me3 mark appearance and decay across meiotic prophase substages.

risks.summary: ChIP-seq profiles are population averages; single-locus timing and direct DSB formation timing are inferred rather than tracked in the same molecule.

claims.text: Four temporal classes of hotspot-associated H3K4me3 marks correlate with DSB repair fate, with early or persistent marks more consistent with crossover-designated sites and fast-turnover marks more consistent with noncrossover repair.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 5-6, Results: PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 signals facilitate the fate of DSBs; Fig. 3
locatorRefs.summary: The authors define early, fast-turnover, slow-turnover, and persistent H3K4me3 classes and link turnover patterns to repair-route timing.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.summary: The model proposes stronger early marks predesignate crossover-fated DSB sites.

risks.summary: The fate assignment is a model supported by timing and known hotspot overlap, not direct lineage tracing of individual DSB outcomes.

claims.text: H3K27ac and H3K36me3 are co-deposited with PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 at a subset of DSB hotspots, while H3K9me2 is locally depleted, forming a PRDM9-coupled chromatin signature.

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF page 6, Results: specific epigenetic marks associated with recombination hotspot formation; Fig. 4
locatorRefs.summary: The paper reports thousands of DSB hotspots co-marked by H3K4me3, H3K36me3, and H3K27ac and Prdm9-dependent loss of the new marks.

risks.summary: Co-deposition and Prdm9 coupling do not prove that each chromatin mark independently controls recombination fate.

claims.text: Open chromatin features, including NDR enrichment and stronger accessibility near early-forming H3K4me3 regions, are associated with DSB sites that are proposed to proceed toward crossover fate.
statistics: two-tailed Student's t-test for accessibility differences

locatorRefs.ref: file:workspace/cell_papers/2020 Refined spatial temporal epigenomic profiling reveals intrinsic__connection between PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 and__the fate of double-stranded breaks.pdf
locatorRefs.locator: PDF pages 6-8, Results: Open chromatin states contribute to the fate decision of DSBs; Fig. 5
locatorRefs.summary: The paper links early H3K4me3 clusters to stronger active marks, NDR enrichment, and higher accessibility.

risks.summary: The authors also note accessibility can depend on Prdm9, Spo11, and Dmc1, so it may be a consequence of DSB progression rather than the upstream cause.

diagnostics: First-pass claims were manually normalized from pdftotext extraction and PDF locators; raw sequencing data were not downloaded or reanalyzed.
The graph intentionally separates direct PRDM9 writer-dependence from the more inferential DSB fate model.
